fuzzer_role.value_profile_cmplog: primary pair A — vpc; carries I2S delta over vp
fuzzer_role.value_profile: primary pair B — vp; baseline without I2S

expected_direction: vpc > vp; vpc/vp ratio grows monotonically with DICT_SIZE

expected_curve_per_fuzzer.value_profile: ≈ 0 at every scan value; (1/2^32)^2 per attempt regardless of DICT_SIZE

acceptance.rule: vpc median > 0 at every DICT_SIZE; vp median ≈ 0 at every DICT_SIZE; vpc curve monotone non-increasing in DICT_SIZE

design_basis: Two-slot tag-table input mirrors lcms _cmsReadHeader's tag-pair iteration. Each slot's 4-byte FOURCC is checked against a DICT_SIZE-entry compile-time literal table via lookup_descriptor(); each entry emits one trace_const_cmp_4 site that I2S logs. Trap requires (group_id equal, value distinct) — a RELATIONAL property of the static-lookup result, which can only be satisfied by chaining TWO dictionary substitutions. vp lacks I2S so cannot land FOURCC bytes in either slot; vpc inherits cmplog's I2S so substitutes both slots and reaches the relational test. The mechanism is qualitatively distinct from i2s_magic_number_gate (single-substitution unlock) because the trap requires a PAIR drawn from the dictionary, not a single literal at a fixed offset.
notes: DICT_SIZE is COMPILE-TIME; one Dockerfile per scan value at libafl_fuzzbench/docker/targets/Dockerfile.b_pair_rel_d{4,16,64,256}.

The dictionary is laid out so consecutive (2k, 2k+1) entries form a group with distinct values; thus DICT_SIZE/2 valid trap-pairs exist. As DICT_SIZE grows, |trap-pairs| grows linearly while |all-pairs| grows quadratically, so resolution rate decays as 1/DICT_SIZE.
Primary pair is (vpc, vp) on the I2S axis — chosen because the real target's strict-wins cell at lcms br56 is on this edge (subject 8 vpc vs vp ΔAUC=23.55M at n=10).

Cousin observation NOT part of this template's claim: at the same branch the (cmplog, naive) edge ALSO shows I2S-helps (subject 5 ΔAUC=17.97M). Same I2S delta direction, but different primary pair. If a separate strict cell on a different real target surfaces with (cmplog, naive) on a pair-relational lookup, it would extend this template via the (cmp, naive) edge; both edges share the I2S mechanism.
